name: QMD MCP Server (stdio + HTTP + daemon)
applicability: Complete MCP server with three transports: stdio (per-client subprocess), HTTP (long-lived), and HTTP daemon (PID-managed). Uses @modelcontextprotocol/sdk 1.29.0. 836 lines.
home_connection: JASON-OS Domain 02a MCP infrastructure. SoNash research confirmed this as direct reference implementation. Shows how to ship an MCP server as a CLI tool with multiple transport options.

name: Claude Code Plugin (marketplace.json)
applicability: Claude Code plugin manifest shipping MCP server + skills bundle. Installable via 'claude plugin marketplace add tobi/qmd'. Single source of truth for plugin discovery, skills path, MCP server config.
home_connection: SoNash could ship its skills ecosystem as a Claude Code plugin. JASON-OS extensibility model. The marketplace.json pattern is portable.

name: QMD Skill with gated tool access
applicability: Claude Code skill with 'allowed-tools: Bash(qmd:*), mcp__qmd__*' frontmatter. Status check via '!`qmd status`' live command execution. 145 lines. References sub-doc 'references/mcp-setup.md' for MCP configuration.
home_connection: SoNash has 81 skills but no SKILL.md with allowed-tools restriction. The gated tool access pattern enhances security. The 'references/' subfolder pattern for skill-specific docs is cleaner than inline bloat.

name: Release Skill (disable-model-invocation)
applicability: Release workflow skill with 'disable-model-invocation: true' — only triggered by explicit user command. Full process: gather context, commit outstanding work, write changelog, cut release, show final, push on confirmation, watch CI via background dispatch. Pins all deps to exact versions.
home_connection: SoNash has release workflows but not a disciplined release SKILL. The 'disable-model-invocation' frontmatter flag prevents Claude from auto-invoking risky operations. Directly applicable to SoNash's release/deploy workflows.

name: QMD Query Syntax (EBNF grammar)
applicability: Formal EBNF grammar for a query DSL with typed sub-queries (lex/vec/hyde), intent disambiguation, and expansion rules. 182 lines with parsing constraints and examples.
home_connection: SoNash's recall skill queries extraction-journal.jsonl with ad-hoc filters. A formal query DSL with typed sub-queries (tag/source/type/candidate) would enable much richer search across 277 candidates.

name: SFT Fine-Tuning Pipeline (HuggingFace Jobs)
applicability: Complete ML training pipeline: LoRA SFT on Qwen3-1.7B, HuggingFace Jobs cloud training ($1.50 per run), GGUF conversion for Ollama deployment, rule-based reward function (5 dimensions max 140 points). 92% eval score.
home_connection: SoNash doesn't fine-tune models currently. Future relevance: if SoNash ever needs a small specialized model (extraction classifier, skill router, query intent), this is the complete blueprint.

name: RRF with position-aware blending
applicability: Reciprocal Rank Fusion (k=60) with original query 2x weight, top-rank bonus (+0.05 for #1, +0.02 for #2-3), then position-aware blend after rerank: 75/60/40% RRF weight for ranks 1-3/4-10/11+.
home_connection: SoNash's synthesize skill ranks candidates across sources but uses no formal fusion algorithm. The RRF + position-aware blend approach is directly applicable to cross-repo candidate ranking in value-map.json aggregation.

name: Smart markdown chunking with scored break points
applicability: Scored break points: H1=100, H2=90, H3=80, code fences=80, paragraphs=20, list items=5, line breaks=1. Squared distance decay: score × (1 - (distance/window)² × 0.7). 900 tokens/chunk with 15% overlap.
home_connection: SoNash's content analysis (repo-analysis, document-analysis) chunks content for storage. Currently uses naive character-based chunks. This scored algorithm respects document structure — directly portable.

name: AST-aware code chunking via tree-sitter
applicability: Tree-sitter parsing for TS/JS/Python/Go/Rust. Chunks at function/class/import boundaries. Merged with regex scores. Markdown always uses regex. Optional via '--chunk-strategy auto'.
home_connection: SoNash's repo-analysis chunks code via repomix but doesn't use AST boundaries. For code-heavy repos, AST-aware chunking would produce better extraction candidates.

name: Search Quality Evaluation Framework
applicability: Easy/medium/hard query categorization with expected docs. Precision@k, recall, MRR, F1 across 4 backends (bm25/vector/hybrid/full). Fixture-based reproducible benchmarks. 'qmd bench' CLI command.
home_connection: SoNash has no formal skill evaluation framework. Skills are tested informally. A fixture-based eval harness with quality metrics would catch skill regressions and enable objective comparison.

name: Multi-runtime CI matrix (Node x Bun x OS)
applicability: Matrix: ubuntu/macos × Node 22/23 + Bun latest. Tests on both runtimes. Nix flake build workflow. Separate publish workflow triggered by tags.
home_connection: SoNash has CI but not cross-runtime testing. Since SoNash scripts target Node, this is lower priority unless Bun support becomes relevant.

name: HyDE: Precise Zero-Shot Dense Retrieval without Relevance Labels
applicability: The HyDE (Hypothetical Document Embeddings) technique used in QMD. Generate a hypothetical answer passage, embed it, search for similar real passages. Works well when you know what the answer should look like.
home_connection: Future SoNash RAG over extraction-journal.jsonl could use HyDE for better semantic match when user asks 'what did we learn about X' — model generates expected answer, embeds, searches.

name: node-llama-cpp for local inference
applicability: Node.js wrapper for llama.cpp. Runs GGUF models locally (embedding, reranking, generation) with GPU acceleration (Metal/CUDA/Vulkan). createRankingContext() + rankAndSort() API for cross-encoder reranking.
home_connection: SoNash has no local inference. If SoNash wants LLM features without API keys (privacy-first vision), node-llama-cpp is the path. Directly relevant to SoNash's privacy-first design principle.

name: sqlite-vec extension
applicability: SQLite extension for vector search. Integrates with better-sqlite3. Vec0 tables for embedding storage. Platform-specific binaries for darwin/linux/windows.

name: Pinned exact versions (no semver ranges)
applicability: All deps pinned to exact versions (e.g., '4.2.1' not '^4.2.1'). Release skill enforces this. Lockfile ensures reproducible installs. Dependency update policy codified in release SKILL.md.
home_connection: SoNash uses caret ranges. For a tool that ships models and compiled extensions (sqlite-vec platform binaries), exact pinning is safer. Pattern applicable to SoNash publishable packages.

name: Daemon mode for model persistence
applicability: MCP HTTP server with --daemon flag. PID file at ~/.cache/qmd/mcp.pid. Keeps ~3GB of models loaded in VRAM across MCP client reconnects. Idle disposal after 5 min.
home_connection: If SoNash ever runs local LLM inference, the daemon pattern avoids expensive model reload per query. Applicable to any resource-heavy service.
